addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: <system-reminder>Warning: the file exists but is shorter than the provided offset (1). The file has 1 lines.</system-reminder>

1	"""Hybrid cross-modal MRI retrieval baseline on Modal.
2	
3	Strategy per dataset:
4	  dataset1 — all images share a common registered grid, so we exploit this
5	              with 3D normalised cross-correlation (NCC) on GPU-downsampled
6	              volumes.  NCC scores are ensembled with DINOv2 cosine similarity
7	              from fine-tuned projection heads.
8	  dataset2/3 — geometric alignment is broken (independent deformations /
9	              pre-op to intra-op shift), so we rely on DINOv2 fine-tuned
10	              projection heads trained on the 350 dataset1 pairs.
11	
12	Run:
13	    modal run laurence/hybrid_modal.py
14	    modal run laurence/hybrid_modal.py --out my_submission.csv
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	import csv
20	import io
21	from pathlib import Path
22	
23	import modal
24	
25	app = modal.App("ehl-hybrid")
26	vol = modal.Volume.from_name("ehl-2026-vol-2")
27	
28	MODEL_ID = "facebook/dinov2-base"
29	BACKBONE_DIM = 768
30	
31	# NCC config (dataset1 only)
32	NCC_SIZE = (48, 48, 48)   # downsample resolution for 3D NCC
33	NCC_WEIGHT = 0.7           # ensemble weight for NCC score
34	DINO_WEIGHT = 0.3          # ensemble weight for DINOv2 score
35	
36	# DINOv2 fine-tune config
37	PROJ_DIM = 256
38	TEMPERATURE = 0.1
39	EPOCHS = 150
40	LR = 3e-3
41	WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4
42	TRAIN_BATCH = 64
43	EMBED_BATCH = 48
44	
45	# Slice extraction
46	SLICE_POSITIONS = (0.35, 0.50, 0.65)
47	IMAGE_SIZE = 224
48	
49	
50	def _download_model():
51	    from transformers import AutoImageProcessor, AutoModel
52	    AutoImageProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
53	    AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
54	
55	
56	image = (
57	    modal.Image.debian_slim(python_version="3.12")
58	    .pip_install(
59	        "torch>=2.3",
60	        "torchvision>=0.18",
61	        "transformers>=4.40",
62	        "nibabel>=5.3",
63	        "numpy>=2.0",
64	        "Pillow>=10.0",
65	    )
66	    .run_function(_download_model)
67	)
68	
69	
70	@app.function(
71	    image=image,
72	    volumes={"/data": vol},
73	    gpu="T4",
74	    timeout=7200,
75	    memory=16384,
76	)
77	def run_hybrid() -> str:
78	    import csv as _csv
79	    import io as _io
80	    from pathlib import Path as _Path
81	
82	    import nibabel as nib
83	    import numpy as np
84	    import torch
85	    import torch.nn as nn
86	    import torch.nn.functional as F
87	    from PIL import Image as PILImage
88	    from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
89	    from transformers import AutoImageProcessor, AutoModel
90	
91	    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
92	    print(f"Device: {device}")
93	
94	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ helpers
95	    data_root = _find_data_root(_Path("/data"))
96	
97	    def read_csv_rows(path: _Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
98	        with path.open(newline="") as f:
99	            return list(_csv.DictReader(f))
100	
101	    def resolve(rel: str) -> _Path:
102	        p = _Path(rel)
103	        p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
104	        if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
105	            alt = p.with_suffix("")
106	            if alt.exists():
107	                return alt
108	        return p
109	
110	    def filter_exists(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str) -> dict[str, _Path]:
111	        out = {k: v for k, v in id_path.items() if v.exists()}
112	        missing = len(id_path) - len(out)
113	        if missing:
114	            print(f"  {label}: {missing} missing paths skipped")
115	        return out
116	
117	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ volume loaders
118	
119	    def load_volume_ncc(nii_path: _Path) -> np.ndarray:
120	        """Load NIfTI, downsample to NCC_SIZE, flatten, return normalised float32 vector."""
121	        img = nib.load(str(nii_path))
122	        arr = img.get_fdata(dtype=np.float32)
123	        if arr.ndim == 4:
124	            arr = arr[..., 0]
125	        arr = np.nan_to_num(arr, nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
126	        # trilinear downsample via torch
127	        t = torch.from_numpy(arr).unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)  # (1,1,X,Y,Z)
128	        t = F.interpolate(t, size=NCC_SIZE, mode="trilinear", align_corners=False)
129	        v = t.squeeze().numpy().flatten().astype(np.float32)
130	        mu, sigma = v.mean(), v.std()
131	        return (v - mu) / (sigma + 1e-6)
132	
133	    def load_pil_slices(nii_path: _Path, all_planes: bool = True) -> list:
134	        """Return PIL RGB slices at representative positions."""
135	        img = nib.load(str(nii_path))
136	        arr = img.get_fdata(dtype=np.float32)
137	        if arr.ndim == 4:
138	            arr = arr[..., 0]
139	        axes = [0, 1, 2] if all_planes else [2]
140	        pils = []
141	        for axis in axes:
142	            a = np.moveaxis(arr, axis, -1)
143	            nz = np.count_nonzero(np.isfinite(a) & (a != 0), axis=(0, 1))
144	            occ = np.where(nz > 0)[0]
145	            lo = int(occ[0]) if len(occ) else 0
146	            hi = int(occ[-1]) if len(occ) else a.shape[2] - 1
147	            for pos in SLICE_POSITIONS:
148	                idx = int(np.clip(round(lo + pos * (hi - lo)), 0, a.shape[2] - 1))
149	                sl = np.nan_to_num(a[:, :, idx], nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
150	                p1, p99 = (np.percentile(sl[sl > 0], (1, 99)) if sl.any() else (0.0, 1.0))
151	                sl = np.clip((sl - p1) / max(p99 - p1, 1e-6) * 255, 0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
152	                pil = PILImage.fromarray(sl).resize((IMAGE_SIZE, IMAGE_SIZE), PILImage.BILINEAR).convert("RGB")
153	                pils.append(pil)
154	        return pils
155	
156	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ collect manifests
157	
158	    prediction_specs = [
159	        ("dataset1", "val"),
160	        ("dataset1", "test"),
161	        ("dataset2", "val"),
162	        ("dataset2", "test"),
163	        ("dataset3", "val"),
164	        ("dataset3", "test"),
165	    ]
166	
167	    all_query_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
168	    all_target_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
169	    prediction_sets: list[dict] = []
170	
171	    for ds, split in prediction_specs:
172	        qcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_queries.csv"
173	        gcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
174	        if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
175	            print(f"Skipping {ds}/{split}: CSV not found")
176	            continue
177	        queries = filter_exists(
178	            {r["query_id"]: resolve(r["query_image"]) for r in read_csv_rows(qcsv)},
179	            f"{ds}/{split} queries",
180	        )
181	        targets = filter_exists(
182	            {r["target_id"]: resolve(r["target_image"]) for r in read_csv_rows(gcsv)},
183	            f"{ds}/{split} targets",
184	        )
185	        if queries and targets:
186	            prediction_sets.append({"ds": ds, "split": split, "queries": queries, "targets": targets})
187	            all_query_paths.update(queries)
188	            all_target_paths.update(targets)
189	            print(f"{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
190	
191	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 1: 3D NCC for dataset1
192	
193	    print("\n=== Stage 1: 3D NCC for dataset1 ===")
194	
195	    dataset1_sets = [ps for ps in prediction_sets if ps["ds"] == "dataset1"]
196	
197	    # collect unique dataset1 image paths
198	    d1_query_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
199	    d1_target_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
200	    for ps in dataset1_sets:
201	        d1_query_paths.update(ps["queries"])
202	        d1_target_paths.update(ps["targets"])
203	
204	    def load_ncc_matrix(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str) -> tuple[list[str], np.ndarray]:
205	        ids = sorted(id_path)
206	        vecs = []
207	        for i, img_id in enumerate(ids):
208	            if i % 20 == 0:
209	                print(f"  {label} {i}/{len(ids)}")
210	            try:
211	                vecs.append(load_volume_ncc(id_path[img_id]))
212	            except Exception as e:
213	                print(f"  ERROR {id_path[img_id]}: {e}")
214	                vecs.append(np.zeros(int(np.prod(NCC_SIZE)), dtype=np.float32))
215	        return ids, np.stack(vecs)  # (N, NCC_SIZE^3)
216	
217	    q1_ids, Q1_ncc = load_ncc_matrix(d1_query_paths, "dataset1 queries")
218	    t1_ids, T1_ncc = load_ncc_matrix(d1_target_paths, "dataset1 targets")
219	
220	    # GPU matrix multiply for NCC scores
221	    Q1_gpu = torch.from_numpy(Q1_ncc).to(device)
222	    T1_gpu = torch.from_numpy(T1_ncc).to(device)
223	    ncc_scores_d1 = (Q1_gpu @ T1_gpu.T / Q1_ncc.shape[1]).cpu().numpy()  # (Nq, Nt)
224	    ncc_q_index = {img_id: i for i, img_id in enumerate(q1_ids)}
225	    ncc_t_index = {img_id: i for i, img_id in enumerate(t1_ids)}
226	    del Q1_gpu, T1_gpu
227	    print(f"NCC matrix: {ncc_scores_d1.shape}, range [{ncc_scores_d1.min():.3f}, {ncc_scores_d1.max():.3f}]")
228	
229	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 2: DINOv2 backbone
230	
231	    print("\n=== Stage 2: DINOv2 frozen feature extraction ===")
232	
233	    processor = AutoImageProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
234	    backbone = AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID).to(device).eval()
235	    for p in backbone.parameters():
236	        p.requires_grad_(False)
237	
238	    @torch.no_grad()
239	    def embed_image_paths(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str, all_planes: bool) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
240	        ids = sorted(id_path)
241	        all_pils: list = []
242	        owners: list[str] = []
243	        print(f"  Loading slices for {len(ids)} {label} images...")
244	        for i, img_id in enumerate(ids):
245	            if i % 50 == 0:
246	                print(f"    {i}/{len(ids)}")
247	            try:
248	                pils = load_pil_slices(id_path[img_id], all_planes=all_planes)
249	            except Exception as e:
250	                print(f"    ERROR {id_path[img_id]}: {e}")
251	                n = 9 if all_planes else 3
252	                pils = [PILImage.fromarray(np.zeros((IMAGE_SIZE, IMAGE_SIZE, 3), dtype=np.uint8))] * n
253	            all_pils.extend(pils)
254	            owners.extend([img_id] * len(pils))
255	
256	        print(f"  Embedding {len(all_pils)} slices...")
257	        cls_list: list[np.ndarray] = []
258	        for start in range(0, len(all_pils), EMBED_BATCH):
259	            batch = all_pils[start : start + EMBED_BATCH]
260	            inputs = processor(images=batch, return_tensors="pt").to(device)
261	            out = backbone(**inputs)
262	            cls_list.append(out.last_hidden_state[:, 0, :].cpu().float().numpy())
263	
264	        all_cls = np.concatenate(cls_list, axis=0)
265	        accum: dict[str, list[np.ndarray]] = {img_id: [] for img_id in ids}
266	        for feat, img_id in zip(all_cls, owners):
267	            accum[img_id].append(feat)
268	
269	        features: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
270	        for img_id, feats in accum.items():
271	            f = np.mean(feats, axis=0).astype(np.float32)
272	            norm = np.linalg.norm(f)
273	            features[img_id] = f / norm if norm > 0 else f
274	        return features
275	
276	    # Training images: axial only (fast)
277	    train_csv = data_root / "dataset1" / "train_pairs.csv"
278	    train_pairs = read_csv_rows(train_csv) if train_csv.exists() else []
279	    train_q_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
280	    train_t_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
281	    valid_pairs: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
282	    for row in train_pairs:
283	        qp, tp = resolve(row["query_image"]), resolve(row["target_image"])
284	        if qp.exists() and tp.exists():
285	            train_q_paths[row["query_id"]] = qp
286	            train_t_paths[row["target_id"]] = tp
287	            valid_pairs.append((row["query_id"], row["target_id"]))
288	    print(f"Training pairs on disk: {len(valid_pairs)}")
289	
290	    train_feats = embed_image_paths({**train_q_paths, **train_t_paths}, "train", all_planes=False)
291	
292	    # Inference images: all 3 planes
293	    infer_q_feats = embed_image_paths(all_query_paths, "query", all_planes=True)
294	    infer_t_feats = embed_image_paths(all_target_paths, "target", all_planes=True)
295	
296	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 3: fine-tune projections
297	
298	    print("\n=== Stage 3: Training projection heads ===")
299	
300	    class ProjectionHead(nn.Module):
301	        def __init__(self) -> None:
302	            super().__init__()
303	            self.net = nn.Sequential(
304	                nn.Linear(BACKBONE_DIM, BACKBONE_DIM),
305	                nn.GELU(),
306	                nn.Linear(BACKBONE_DIM, PROJ_DIM),
307	            )
308	
309	        def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
310	            return F.normalize(self.net(x), dim=-1)
311	
312	    query_head = ProjectionHead().to(device)
313	    target_head = ProjectionHead().to(device)
314	
315	    q_vecs = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([train_feats[qid] for qid, _ in valid_pairs])).to(device)
316	    t_vecs = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([train_feats[tid] for _, tid in valid_pairs])).to(device)
317	    loader = DataLoader(TensorDataset(q_vecs, t_vecs), batch_size=TRAIN_BATCH, shuffle=True)
318	
319	    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
320	        list(query_head.parameters()) + list(target_head.parameters()),
321	        lr=LR, weight_decay=WEIGHT_DECAY,
322	    )
323	    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=EPOCHS)
324	
325	    for epoch in range(1, EPOCHS + 1):
326	        query_head.train(); target_head.train()
327	        total_loss = 0.0; n_seen = 0
328	        for qb, tb in loader:
329	            q_emb = query_head(qb)
330	            t_emb = target_head(tb)
331	            logits = q_emb @ t_emb.T / TEMPERATURE
332	            labels = torch.arange(len(q_emb), device=device)
333	            loss = (F.cross_entropy(logits, labels) + F.cross_entropy(logits.T, labels)) / 2
334	            optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
335	            loss.backward()
336	            nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(
337	                list(query_head.parameters()) + list(target_head.parameters()), 1.0
338	            )
339	            optimizer.step()
340	            total_loss += loss.item() * len(qb); n_seen += len(qb)
341	        scheduler.step()
342	        if epoch % 25 == 0 or epoch == 1:
343	            print(f"  epoch {epoch:03d}/{EPOCHS}  loss={total_loss / n_seen:.4f}")
344	
345	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 4: project + rank
346	
347	    print("\n=== Stage 4: Projecting and ranking ===")
348	    query_head.eval(); target_head.eval()
349	
350	    @torch.no_grad()
351	    def project(feats: dict[str, np.ndarray], head: nn.Module) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
352	        ids = sorted(feats)
353	        vecs = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([feats[i] for i in ids])).to(device)
354	        embs = head(vecs).cpu().numpy()
355	        return {img_id: embs[j] for j, img_id in enumerate(ids)}
356	
357	    proj_q = project(infer_q_feats, query_head)
358	    proj_t = project(infer_t_feats, target_head)
359	
360	    submission_rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
361	
362	    for pred_set in prediction_sets:
363	        ds = pred_set["ds"]
364	        query_ids = [qid for qid in sorted(pred_set["queries"]) if qid in proj_q]
365	        target_ids = [tid for tid in sorted(pred_set["targets"]) if tid in proj_t]
366	        if not query_ids or not target_ids:
367	            continue

372

"""Hybrid cross-modal MRI retrieval baseline on Modal.

Strategy per dataset:
  dataset1 — all images share a common registered grid, so we exploit this
              with 3D normalised cross-correlation (NCC) on GPU-downsampled
              volumes.  NCC scores are ensembled with DINOv2 cosine similarity
              from fine-tuned projection heads.
  dataset2/3 — geometric alignment is broken (independent deformations /
              pre-op to intra-op shift), so we rely on DINOv2 fine-tuned
              projection heads trained on the 350 dataset1 pairs.

Run:
    modal run laurence/hybrid_modal.py
    modal run laurence/hybrid_modal.py --out my_submission.csv
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import io
from pathlib import Path

import modal

app = modal.App("ehl-hybrid")
vol = modal.Volume.from_name("ehl-2026-vol-2")

MODEL_ID = "facebook/dinov2-base"
BACKBONE_DIM = 768

# NCC config (dataset1 only)
NCC_SIZE = (48, 48, 48)   # downsample resolution for 3D NCC
NCC_WEIGHT = 0.7           # ensemble weight for NCC score
DINO_WEIGHT = 0.3          # ensemble weight for DINOv2 score

# DINOv2 fine-tune config
PROJ_DIM = 256
TEMPERATURE = 0.1
EPOCHS = 150
LR = 3e-3
WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4
TRAIN_BATCH = 64
EMBED_BATCH = 48

# Slice extraction
SLICE_POSITIONS = (0.35, 0.50, 0.65)
IMAGE_SIZE = 224


def _download_model():
    from transformers import AutoImageProcessor, AutoModel
    AutoImageProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
    AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)


image = (
    modal.Image.debian_slim(python_version="3.12")
    .pip_install(
        "torch>=2.3",
        "torchvision>=0.18",
        "transformers>=4.40",
        "nibabel>=5.3",
        "numpy>=2.0",
        "Pillow>=10.0",
    )
    .run_function(_download_model)
)


@app.function(
    image=image,
    volumes={"/data": vol},
    gpu="T4",
    timeout=7200,
    memory=16384,
)
def run_hybrid() -> str:
    import csv as _csv
    import io as _io
    from pathlib import Path as _Path

    import nibabel as nib
    import numpy as np
    import torch
    import torch.nn as nn
    import torch.nn.functional as F
    from PIL import Image as PILImage
    from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
    from transformers import AutoImageProcessor, AutoModel

    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
    print(f"Device: {device}")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ helpers
    data_root = _find_data_root(_Path("/data"))

    def read_csv_rows(path: _Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
        with path.open(newline="") as f:
            return list(_csv.DictReader(f))

    def resolve(rel: str) -> _Path:
        p = _Path(rel)
        p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
        if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
            alt = p.with_suffix("")
            if alt.exists():
                return alt
        return p

    def filter_exists(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str) -> dict[str, _Path]:
        out = {k: v for k, v in id_path.items() if v.exists()}
        missing = len(id_path) - len(out)
        if missing:
            print(f"  {label}: {missing} missing paths skipped")
        return out

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ volume loaders

    def load_volume_ncc(nii_path: _Path) -> np.ndarray:
        """Load NIfTI, downsample to NCC_SIZE, flatten, return normalised float32 vector."""
        img = nib.load(str(nii_path))
        arr = img.get_fdata(dtype=np.float32)
        if arr.ndim == 4:
            arr = arr[..., 0]
        arr = np.nan_to_num(arr, nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
        # trilinear downsample via torch
        t = torch.from_numpy(arr).unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)  # (1,1,X,Y,Z)
        t = F.interpolate(t, size=NCC_SIZE, mode="trilinear", align_corners=False)
        v = t.squeeze().numpy().flatten().astype(np.float32)
        mu, sigma = v.mean(), v.std()
        return (v - mu) / (sigma + 1e-6)

    def load_pil_slices(nii_path: _Path, all_planes: bool = True) -> list:
        """Return PIL RGB slices at representative positions."""
        img = nib.load(str(nii_path))
        arr = img.get_fdata(dtype=np.float32)
        if arr.ndim == 4:
            arr = arr[..., 0]
        axes = [0, 1, 2] if all_planes else [2]
        pils = []
        for axis in axes:
            a = np.moveaxis(arr, axis, -1)
            nz = np.count_nonzero(np.isfinite(a) & (a != 0), axis=(0, 1))
            occ = np.where(nz > 0)[0]
            lo = int(occ[0]) if len(occ) else 0
            hi = int(occ[-1]) if len(occ) else a.shape[2] - 1
            for pos in SLICE_POSITIONS:
                idx = int(np.clip(round(lo + pos * (hi - lo)), 0, a.shape[2] - 1))
                sl = np.nan_to_num(a[:, :, idx], nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
                p1, p99 = (np.percentile(sl[sl > 0], (1, 99)) if sl.any() else (0.0, 1.0))
                sl = np.clip((sl - p1) / max(p99 - p1, 1e-6) * 255, 0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
                pil = PILImage.fromarray(sl).resize((IMAGE_SIZE, IMAGE_SIZE), PILImage.BILINEAR).convert("RGB")
                pils.append(pil)
        return pils

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ collect manifests

    prediction_specs = [
        ("dataset1", "val"),
        ("dataset1", "test"),
        ("dataset2", "val"),
        ("dataset2", "test"),
        ("dataset3", "val"),
        ("dataset3", "test"),
    ]

    all_query_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
    all_target_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
    prediction_sets: list[dict] = []

    for ds, split in prediction_specs:
        qcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_queries.csv"
        gcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
        if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
            print(f"Skipping {ds}/{split}: CSV not found")
            continue
        queries = filter_exists(
            {r["query_id"]: resolve(r["query_image"]) for r in read_csv_rows(qcsv)},
            f"{ds}/{split} queries",
        )
        targets = filter_exists(
            {r["target_id"]: resolve(r["target_image"]) for r in read_csv_rows(gcsv)},
            f"{ds}/{split} targets",
        )
        if queries and targets:
            prediction_sets.append({"ds": ds, "split": split, "queries": queries, "targets": targets})
            all_query_paths.update(queries)
            all_target_paths.update(targets)
            print(f"{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 1: 3D NCC for dataset1

    print("\n=== Stage 1: 3D NCC for dataset1 ===")

    dataset1_sets = [ps for ps in prediction_sets if ps["ds"] == "dataset1"]

    # collect unique dataset1 image paths
    d1_query_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
    d1_target_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
    for ps in dataset1_sets:
        d1_query_paths.update(ps["queries"])
        d1_target_paths.update(ps["targets"])

    def load_ncc_matrix(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str) -> tuple[list[str], np.ndarray]:
        ids = sorted(id_path)
        vecs = []
        for i, img_id in enumerate(ids):
            if i % 20 == 0:
                print(f"  {label} {i}/{len(ids)}")
            try:
                vecs.append(load_volume_ncc(id_path[img_id]))
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"  ERROR {id_path[img_id]}: {e}")
                vecs.append(np.zeros(int(np.prod(NCC_SIZE)), dtype=np.float32))
        return ids, np.stack(vecs)  # (N, NCC_SIZE^3)

    q1_ids, Q1_ncc = load_ncc_matrix(d1_query_paths, "dataset1 queries")
    t1_ids, T1_ncc = load_ncc_matrix(d1_target_paths, "dataset1 targets")

    # GPU matrix multiply for NCC scores
    Q1_gpu = torch.from_numpy(Q1_ncc).to(device)
    T1_gpu = torch.from_numpy(T1_ncc).to(device)
    ncc_scores_d1 = (Q1_gpu @ T1_gpu.T / Q1_ncc.shape[1]).cpu().numpy()  # (Nq, Nt)
    ncc_q_index = {img_id: i for i, img_id in enumerate(q1_ids)}
    ncc_t_index = {img_id: i for i, img_id in enumerate(t1_ids)}
    del Q1_gpu, T1_gpu
    print(f"NCC matrix: {ncc_scores_d1.shape}, range [{ncc_scores_d1.min():.3f}, {ncc_scores_d1.max():.3f}]")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 2: DINOv2 backbone

    print("\n=== Stage 2: DINOv2 frozen feature extraction ===")

    processor = AutoImageProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
    backbone = AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID).to(device).eval()
    for p in backbone.parameters():
        p.requires_grad_(False)

    @torch.no_grad()
    def embed_image_paths(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str, all_planes: bool) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
        ids = sorted(id_path)
        all_pils: list = []
        owners: list[str] = []
        print(f"  Loading slices for {len(ids)} {label} images...")
        for i, img_id in enumerate(ids):
            if i % 50 == 0:
                print(f"    {i}/{len(ids)}")
            try:
                pils = load_pil_slices(id_path[img_id], all_planes=all_planes)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"    ERROR {id_path[img_id]}: {e}")
                n = 9 if all_planes else 3
                pils = [PILImage.fromarray(np.zeros((IMAGE_SIZE, IMAGE_SIZE, 3), dtype=np.uint8))] * n
            all_pils.extend(pils)
            owners.extend([img_id] * len(pils))

        print(f"  Embedding {len(all_pils)} slices...")
        cls_list: list[np.ndarray] = []
        for start in range(0, len(all_pils), EMBED_BATCH):
            batch = all_pils[start : start + EMBED_BATCH]
            inputs = processor(images=batch, return_tensors="pt").to(device)
            out = backbone(**inputs)
            cls_list.append(out.last_hidden_state[:, 0, :].cpu().float().numpy())

        all_cls = np.concatenate(cls_list, axis=0)
        accum: dict[str, list[np.ndarray]] = {img_id: [] for img_id in ids}
        for feat, img_id in zip(all_cls, owners):
            accum[img_id].append(feat)

        features: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
        for img_id, feats in accum.items():
            f = np.mean(feats, axis=0).astype(np.float32)
            norm = np.linalg.norm(f)
            features[img_id] = f / norm if norm > 0 else f
        return features

    # Training images: axial only (fast)
    train_csv = data_root / "dataset1" / "train_pairs.csv"
    train_pairs = read_csv_rows(train_csv) if train_csv.exists() else []
    train_q_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
    train_t_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
    valid_pairs: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    for row in train_pairs:
        qp, tp = resolve(row["query_image"]), resolve(row["target_image"])
        if qp.exists() and tp.exists():
            train_q_paths[row["query_id"]] = qp
            train_t_paths[row["target_id"]] = tp
            valid_pairs.append((row["query_id"], row["target_id"]))
    print(f"Training pairs on disk: {len(valid_pairs)}")

    train_feats = embed_image_paths({**train_q_paths, **train_t_paths}, "train", all_planes=False)

    # Inference images: all 3 planes
    infer_q_feats = embed_image_paths(all_query_paths, "query", all_planes=True)
    infer_t_feats = embed_image_paths(all_target_paths, "target", all_planes=True)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 3: fine-tune projections

    print("\n=== Stage 3: Training projection heads ===")

    class ProjectionHead(nn.Module):
        def __init__(self) -> None:
            super().__init__()
            self.net = nn.Sequential(
                nn.Linear(BACKBONE_DIM, BACKBONE_DIM),
                nn.GELU(),
                nn.Linear(BACKBONE_DIM, PROJ_DIM),
            )

        def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
            return F.normalize(self.net(x), dim=-1)

    query_head = ProjectionHead().to(device)
    target_head = ProjectionHead().to(device)

    q_vecs = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([train_feats[qid] for qid, _ in valid_pairs])).to(device)
    t_vecs = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([train_feats[tid] for _, tid in valid_pairs])).to(device)
    loader = DataLoader(TensorDataset(q_vecs, t_vecs), batch_size=TRAIN_BATCH, shuffle=True)

    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
        list(query_head.parameters()) + list(target_head.parameters()),
        lr=LR, weight_decay=WEIGHT_DECAY,
    )
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=EPOCHS)

    for epoch in range(1, EPOCHS + 1):
        query_head.train(); target_head.train()
        total_loss = 0.0; n_seen = 0
        for qb, tb in loader:
            q_emb = query_head(qb)
            t_emb = target_head(tb)
            logits = q_emb @ t_emb.T / TEMPERATURE
            labels = torch.arange(len(q_emb), device=device)
            loss = (F.cross_entropy(logits, labels) + F.cross_entropy(logits.T, labels)) / 2
            optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
            loss.backward()
            nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(
                list(query_head.parameters()) + list(target_head.parameters()), 1.0
            )
            optimizer.step()
            total_loss += loss.item() * len(qb); n_seen += len(qb)
        scheduler.step()
        if epoch % 25 == 0 or epoch == 1:
            print(f"  epoch {epoch:03d}/{EPOCHS}  loss={total_loss / n_seen:.4f}")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 4: project + rank

    print("\n=== Stage 4: Projecting and ranking ===")
    query_head.eval(); target_head.eval()

    @torch.no_grad()
    def project(feats: dict[str, np.ndarray], head: nn.Module) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
        ids = sorted(feats)
        vecs = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([feats[i] for i in ids])).to(device)
        embs = head(vecs).cpu().numpy()
        return {img_id: embs[j] for j, img_id in enumerate(ids)}

    proj_q = project(infer_q_feats, query_head)
    proj_t = project(infer_t_feats, target_head)

    submission_rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []

    for pred_set in prediction_sets:
        ds = pred_set["ds"]
        query_ids = [qid for qid in sorted(pred_set["queries"]) if qid in proj_q]
        target_ids = [tid for tid in sorted(pred_set["targets"]) if tid in proj_t]
        if not query_ids or not target_ids:
            continue

        Q = np.stack([proj_q[qid] for qid in query_ids])
        T = np.stack([proj_t[tid] for tid in target_ids])
        dino_scores = Q @ T.T  # (Nq, Nt), cosine similarity

        if ds == "dataset1":
            # Build NCC score sub-matrix for this prediction set
            ncc_q_idx = [ncc_q_index[qid] for qid in query_ids]
            ncc_t_idx = [ncc_t_index[tid] for tid in target_ids]
            ncc_sub = ncc_scores_d1[np.ix_(ncc_q_idx, ncc_t_idx)]

            # Normalise each score matrix to [0,1] per query before ensembling
            def minmax_rows(m: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
                lo = m.min(axis=1, keepdims=True)
                hi = m.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)
                return (m - lo) / np.where(hi > lo, hi - lo, 1.0)

            scores = NCC_WEIGHT * minmax_rows(ncc_sub) + DINO_WEIGHT * minmax_rows(dino_scores)
        else:
            scores = dino_scores

        for i, qid in enumerate(query_ids):
            ranked = [target_ids[j] for j in np.argsort(-scores[i])]
            submission_rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranked)})

    buf = _io.StringIO()
    writer = _csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=["query_id", "target_id_ranking"])
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerows(submission_rows)
    print(f"\nGenerated {len(submission_rows)} submission rows.")
    return buf.getvalue()


def _find_data_root(mount: Path) -> Path:
    for p in sorted(mount.rglob("dataset1")):
        if p.is_dir():
            found = p.parent
            print(f"Found data root: {found}")
            return found
    raise RuntimeError(f"Could not find dataset1/ under {mount}. Top-level: {list(mount.iterdir())}")


@app.local_entrypoint()
def main(out: str = "hybrid_submission.csv") -> None:
    print("Running hybrid baseline on Modal...")
    csv_content = run_hybrid.remote()
    out_path = Path(out)
    out_path.write_text(csv_content, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"Saved {len(csv_content.splitlines()) - 1} rows to {out_path}")

1	"""RadDINO + augmented contrastive fine-tuning + alpha-QE re-ranking on Modal.
2	
3	Three improvements over hybrid_modal.py:
4	
5	  1. RadDINO (microsoft/rad-dino) — same ViT-B architecture as DINOv2 but
6	     pre-trained on 1.6M radiology images with DINO objective.  Much better
7	     starting representations for MRI than natural-image DINOv2.
8	
9	  2. Augmented training — for each training image, K independent augmented
10	     versions are pre-computed through the frozen backbone (random flips,
11	     90°-multiples rotation, intensity jitter applied to the 2D slices before
12	     passing through RadDINO).  Each (aug_query, aug_target) combination becomes
13	     a valid positive pair.  This multiplies the effective training set by K²
14	     and teaches the projection heads to produce geometry-invariant embeddings,
15	     directly addressing dataset2's independent deformations.
16	
17	  3. Alpha-QE re-ranking — after initial ranking, each query embedding is
18	     expanded with the mean of its top-k matched target embeddings.  The
19	     expanded (re-normalised) query is used for a final re-rank.  Free
20	     accuracy gain on top of any embedding.
21	
22	  Dataset1 still gets the 3D NCC + DINOv2 ensemble.
23	
24	Run:
25	    modal run laurence/rad_dino_modal.py
26	    modal run laurence/rad_dino_modal.py --out my_submission.csv
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import csv
32	import io
33	from pathlib import Path
34	
35	import modal
36	
37	app = modal.App("ehl-rad-dino")
38	vol = modal.Volume.from_name("ehl-2026-vol-2")
39	
40	MODEL_ID = "microsoft/rad-dino"
41	BACKBONE_DIM = 768
42	
43	# projection head
44	PROJ_DIM = 256
45	TEMPERATURE = 0.1
46	EPOCHS = 150
47	LR = 3e-3
48	WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4
49	TRAIN_BATCH = 64
50	EMBED_BATCH = 48
51	
52	# augmentation
53	K_AUG = 4          # independent augmented views per training image
54	
55	# alpha-QE re-ranking
56	AQE_K = 5          # top-k neighbours used to expand the query
57	AQE_ALPHA = 0.5    # weight of original query vs neighbour mean
58	
59	# 3D NCC (dataset1 only)
60	NCC_SIZE = (48, 48, 48)
61	NCC_WEIGHT = 0.7
62	DINO_WEIGHT = 0.3
63	
64	SLICE_POSITIONS = (0.35, 0.50, 0.65)
65	IMAGE_SIZE = 224
66	
67	
68	def _download_model():
69	    from transformers import AutoImageProcessor, AutoModel
70	    AutoImageProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
71	    AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
72	
73	
74	image = (
75	    modal.Image.debian_slim(python_version="3.12")
76	    .pip_install(
77	        "torch>=2.3",
78	        "torchvision>=0.18",
79	        "transformers>=4.40",
80	        "nibabel>=5.3",
81	        "numpy>=2.0",
82	        "Pillow>=10.0",
83	    )
84	    .run_function(_download_model)
85	)
86	
87	
88	@app.function(
89	    image=image,
90	    volumes={"/data": vol},
91	    gpu="T4",
92	    timeout=7200,
93	    memory=16384,
94	)
95	def run_rad_dino() -> str:
96	    import csv as _csv
97	    import io as _io
98	    import random as _random
99	    from pathlib import Path as _Path
100	
101	    import nibabel as nib
102	    import numpy as np
103	    import torch
104	    import torch.nn as nn
105	    import torch.nn.functional as F
106	    import torchvision.transforms.functional as TF
107	    from PIL import Image as PILImage
108	    from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
109	    from transformers import AutoImageProcessor, AutoModel
110	
111	    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
112	    print(f"Device: {device}")
113	
114	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ helpers
115	
116	    data_root = _find_data_root(_Path("/data"))
117	
118	    def read_csv_rows(path: _Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
119	        with path.open(newline="") as f:
120	            return list(_csv.DictReader(f))
121	
122	    def resolve(rel: str) -> _Path:
123	        p = _Path(rel)
124	        p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
125	        if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
126	            alt = p.with_suffix("")
127	            if alt.exists():
128	                return alt
129	        return p
130	
131	    def filter_exists(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str) -> dict[str, _Path]:
132	        out = {k: v for k, v in id_path.items() if v.exists()}
133	        if len(out) < len(id_path):
134	            print(f"  {label}: {len(id_path) - len(out)} paths missing")
135	        return out
136	
137	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ slice loader
138	
139	    def volume_to_pils(nii_path: _Path, all_planes: bool) -> list:
140	        """Load NIfTI and return PIL RGB slices."""
141	        img = nib.load(str(nii_path))
142	        arr = img.get_fdata(dtype=np.float32)
143	        if arr.ndim == 4:
144	            arr = arr[..., 0]
145	        axes = [0, 1, 2] if all_planes else [2]
146	        pils = []
147	        for axis in axes:
148	            a = np.moveaxis(arr, axis, -1)
149	            nz = np.count_nonzero(np.isfinite(a) & (a != 0), axis=(0, 1))
150	            occ = np.where(nz > 0)[0]
151	            lo = int(occ[0]) if len(occ) else 0
152	            hi = int(occ[-1]) if len(occ) else a.shape[2] - 1
153	            for pos in SLICE_POSITIONS:
154	                idx = int(np.clip(round(lo + pos * (hi - lo)), 0, a.shape[2] - 1))
155	                sl = np.nan_to_num(a[:, :, idx], nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
156	                p1, p99 = (np.percentile(sl[sl > 0], (1, 99)) if sl.any() else (0.0, 1.0))
157	                sl = np.clip((sl - p1) / max(p99 - p1, 1e-6) * 255, 0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
158	                pil = PILImage.fromarray(sl).resize((IMAGE_SIZE, IMAGE_SIZE), PILImage.BILINEAR).convert("RGB")
159	                pils.append(pil)
160	        return pils
161	
162	    def augment_pil(pil: PILImage.Image) -> PILImage.Image:
163	        """Random flip, 90°-multiple rotation, and intensity jitter."""
164	        if _random.random() > 0.5:
165	            pil = TF.hflip(pil)
166	        if _random.random() > 0.5:
167	            pil = TF.vflip(pil)
168	        pil = TF.rotate(pil, _random.choice([0, 90, 180, 270]))
169	        pil = TF.adjust_brightness(pil, _random.uniform(0.75, 1.25))
170	        pil = TF.adjust_contrast(pil, _random.uniform(0.75, 1.25))
171	        return pil
172	
173	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ backbone
174	
175	    processor = AutoImageProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID)
176	    backbone = AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID).to(device).eval()
177	    for p in backbone.parameters():
178	        p.requires_grad_(False)
179	
180	    @torch.no_grad()
181	    def embed_pils(pils: list, owners: list[str]) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
182	        """Run RadDINO on a flat list of PIL images, average per owner id."""
183	        all_cls: list[np.ndarray] = []
184	        for start in range(0, len(pils), EMBED_BATCH):
185	            batch = pils[start : start + EMBED_BATCH]
186	            inputs = processor(images=batch, return_tensors="pt").to(device)
187	            out = backbone(**inputs)
188	            all_cls.append(out.last_hidden_state[:, 0, :].cpu().float().numpy())
189	        all_cls_np = np.concatenate(all_cls, axis=0)
190	
191	        accum: dict[str, list[np.ndarray]] = {}
192	        for feat, owner in zip(all_cls_np, owners):
193	            accum.setdefault(owner, []).append(feat)
194	
195	        features: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
196	        for img_id, feats in accum.items():
197	            f = np.mean(feats, axis=0).astype(np.float32)
198	            norm = np.linalg.norm(f)
199	            features[img_id] = f / norm if norm > 0 else f
200	        return features
201	
202	    def extract_features(
203	        id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str, all_planes: bool, n_aug: int = 1
204	    ) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
205	        """
206	        Extract and average RadDINO CLS features.
207	        n_aug=1 → no augmentation (inference mode).
208	        n_aug>1 → n_aug independent augmented passes averaged (training mode).
209	        """
210	        ids = sorted(id_path)
211	        all_pils: list = []
212	        owners: list[str] = []
213	        print(f"  Slicing {len(ids)} {label} volumes (planes={'all' if all_planes else 'axial'}, aug={n_aug})...")
214	        for i, img_id in enumerate(ids):
215	            if i % 50 == 0:
216	                print(f"    {i}/{len(ids)}")
217	            try:
218	                base_pils = volume_to_pils(id_path[img_id], all_planes=all_planes)
219	            except Exception as e:
220	                print(f"    ERROR {id_path[img_id]}: {e}")
221	                n = (9 if all_planes else 3) * n_aug
222	                base_pils = [PILImage.fromarray(np.zeros((IMAGE_SIZE, IMAGE_SIZE, 3), dtype=np.uint8))] * n
223	                all_pils.extend(base_pils)
224	                owners.extend([img_id] * n)
225	                continue
226	
227	            for _ in range(n_aug):
228	                for pil in base_pils:
229	                    all_pils.append(augment_pil(pil) if n_aug > 1 else pil)
230	                    owners.append(img_id)
231	
232	        print(f"  Embedding {len(all_pils)} slices through RadDINO...")
233	        return embed_pils(all_pils, owners)
234	
235	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ collect manifests
236	
237	    prediction_specs = [
238	        ("dataset1", "val"),
239	        ("dataset1", "test"),
240	        ("dataset2", "val"),
241	        ("dataset2", "test"),
242	        ("dataset3", "val"),
243	        ("dataset3", "test"),
244	    ]
245	
246	    all_query_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
247	    all_target_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
248	    prediction_sets: list[dict] = []
249	
250	    for ds, split in prediction_specs:
251	        qcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_queries.csv"
252	        gcsv = data_root / ds / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
253	        if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
254	            print(f"Skipping {ds}/{split}: CSV not found")
255	            continue
256	        queries = filter_exists(
257	            {r["query_id"]: resolve(r["query_image"]) for r in read_csv_rows(qcsv)},
258	            f"{ds}/{split} queries",
259	        )
260	        targets = filter_exists(
261	            {r["target_id"]: resolve(r["target_image"]) for r in read_csv_rows(gcsv)},
262	            f"{ds}/{split} targets",
263	        )
264	        if queries and targets:
265	            prediction_sets.append({"ds": ds, "split": split, "queries": queries, "targets": targets})
266	            all_query_paths.update(queries)
267	            all_target_paths.update(targets)
268	            print(f"{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
269	
270	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 1: 3D NCC (dataset1)
271	
272	    print("\n=== Stage 1: 3D NCC for dataset1 ===")
273	
274	    d1_query_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
275	    d1_target_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
276	    for ps in prediction_sets:
277	        if ps["ds"] == "dataset1":
278	            d1_query_paths.update(ps["queries"])
279	            d1_target_paths.update(ps["targets"])
280	
281	    def load_ncc_vecs(id_path: dict[str, _Path], label: str) -> tuple[list[str], np.ndarray]:
282	        ids = sorted(id_path)
283	        vecs = []
284	        for i, img_id in enumerate(ids):
285	            if i % 20 == 0:
286	                print(f"  {label} {i}/{len(ids)}")
287	            try:
288	                img = nib.load(str(id_path[img_id]))
289	                arr = img.get_fdata(dtype=np.float32)
290	                if arr.ndim == 4:
291	                    arr = arr[..., 0]
292	                arr = np.nan_to_num(arr, nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
293	                t = torch.from_numpy(arr).unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)
294	                t = F.interpolate(t, size=NCC_SIZE, mode="trilinear", align_corners=False)
295	                v = t.squeeze().numpy().flatten().astype(np.float32)
296	            except Exception as e:
297	                print(f"  ERROR {id_path[img_id]}: {e}")
298	                v = np.zeros(int(np.prod(NCC_SIZE)), dtype=np.float32)
299	            mu, sigma = v.mean(), v.std()
300	            vecs.append((v - mu) / (sigma + 1e-6))
301	        return ids, np.stack(vecs)
302	
303	    q1_ids, Q1_ncc = load_ncc_vecs(d1_query_paths, "dataset1 queries")
304	    t1_ids, T1_ncc = load_ncc_vecs(d1_target_paths, "dataset1 targets")
305	
306	    Q1_gpu = torch.from_numpy(Q1_ncc).to(device)
307	    T1_gpu = torch.from_numpy(T1_ncc).to(device)
308	    ncc_scores_d1 = (Q1_gpu @ T1_gpu.T / Q1_ncc.shape[1]).cpu().numpy()
309	    ncc_q_idx = {img_id: i for i, img_id in enumerate(q1_ids)}
310	    ncc_t_idx = {img_id: i for i, img_id in enumerate(t1_ids)}
311	    del Q1_gpu, T1_gpu
312	    print(f"NCC matrix: {ncc_scores_d1.shape}")
313	
314	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 2: RadDINO features
315	
316	    print("\n=== Stage 2: RadDINO feature extraction ===")
317	
318	    train_csv = data_root / "dataset1" / "train_pairs.csv"
319	    train_pairs_rows = read_csv_rows(train_csv) if train_csv.exists() else []
320	    train_q_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
321	    train_t_paths: dict[str, _Path] = {}
322	    valid_pairs: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
323	    for row in train_pairs_rows:
324	        qp, tp = resolve(row["query_image"]), resolve(row["target_image"])
325	        if qp.exists() and tp.exists():
326	            train_q_paths[row["query_id"]] = qp
327	            train_t_paths[row["target_id"]] = tp
328	            valid_pairs.append((row["query_id"], row["target_id"]))
329	    print(f"Training pairs: {len(valid_pairs)}")
330	
331	    # Training: K augmented views per image, axial slices only
332	    print("Extracting augmented training features...")
333	    train_q_aug_feats: list[dict[str, np.ndarray]] = []
334	    train_t_aug_feats: list[dict[str, np.ndarray]] = []
335	    for k in range(K_AUG):
336	        print(f"  Augmentation {k + 1}/{K_AUG}")
337	        _random.seed(k)
338	        train_q_aug_feats.append(extract_features(train_q_paths, f"train-query-aug{k}", all_planes=False, n_aug=1))
339	        _random.seed(k + 100)
340	        train_t_aug_feats.append(extract_features(train_t_paths, f"train-target-aug{k}", all_planes=False, n_aug=1))
341	
342	    # Inference: no augmentation, all 3 planes
343	    print("Extracting inference features (all planes, no aug)...")
344	    infer_q_feats = extract_features(all_query_paths, "queries", all_planes=True, n_aug=1)
345	    infer_t_feats = extract_features(all_target_paths, "targets", all_planes=True, n_aug=1)
346	
347	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 3: projection heads
348	
349	    print("\n=== Stage 3: Training projection heads ===")
350	
351	    class ProjectionHead(nn.Module):
352	        def __init__(self) -> None:
353	            super().__init__()
354	            self.net = nn.Sequential(
355	                nn.Linear(BACKBONE_DIM, BACKBONE_DIM),
356	                nn.GELU(),
357	                nn.LayerNorm(BACKBONE_DIM),
358	                nn.Linear(BACKBONE_DIM, PROJ_DIM),
359	            )
360	
361	        def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
362	            return F.normalize(self.net(x), dim=-1)
363	
364	    query_head = ProjectionHead().to(device)
365	    target_head = ProjectionHead().to(device)
366	
367	    # Build augmented training tensors: all K_AUG combinations of (q_aug_i, t_aug_j)
368	    q_vecs_list, t_vecs_list = [], []
369	    for q_feats in train_q_aug_feats:
370	        for t_feats in train_t_aug_feats:
371	            for qid, tid in valid_pairs:
372	                if qid in q_feats and tid in t_feats:
373	                    q_vecs_list.append(q_feats[qid])
374	                    t_vecs_list.append(t_feats[tid])
375	
376	    q_tensor = torch.from_numpy(np.stack(q_vecs_list)).to(device)
377	    t_tensor = torch.from_numpy(np.stack(t_vecs_list)).to(device)
378	    print(f"Effective training pairs (with augmentation): {len(q_vecs_list)}")
379	
380	    loader = DataLoader(TensorDataset(q_tensor, t_tensor), batch_size=TRAIN_BATCH, shuffle=True)
381	    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
382	        list(query_head.parameters()) + list(target_head.parameters()),
383	        lr=LR, weight_decay=WEIGHT_DECAY,
384	    )
385	    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=EPOCHS)
386	
387	    for epoch in range(1, EPOCHS + 1):
388	        query_head.train(); target_head.train()
389	        total_loss = 0.0; n_seen = 0
390	        for qb, tb in loader:
391	            q_emb = query_head(qb)
392	            t_emb = target_head(tb)
393	            logits = q_emb @ t_emb.T / TEMPERATURE
394	            labels = torch.arange(len(q_emb), device=device)
395	            loss = (F.cross_entropy(logits, labels) + F.cross_entropy(logits.T, labels)) / 2
396	            optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
397	            loss.backward()
398	            nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(
399	                list(query_head.parameters()) + list(target_head.parameters()), 1.0
400	            )
401	            optimizer.step()
402	            total_loss += loss.item() * len(qb); n_seen += len(qb)
403	        scheduler.step()
404	        if epoch % 25 == 0 or epoch == 1:
405	            print(f"  epoch {epoch:03d}/{EPOCHS}  loss={total_loss / n_seen:.4f}")
406	
407	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ stage 4: project + alpha-QE + rank
408	
409	    print("\n=== Stage 4: Projecting, re-ranking, and generating submission ===")
410	    query_head.eval(); target_head.eval()
411	
412	    @torch.no_grad()
413	    def project(feats: dict[str, np.ndarray], head: nn.Module) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
414	        ids = sorted(feats)
415	        vecs = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([feats[i] for i in ids])).to(device)
416	        embs = head(vecs).cpu().numpy()
417	        return {img_id: embs[j] for j, img_id in enumerate(ids)}
418	
419	    proj_q = project(infer_q_feats, query_head)
420	    proj_t = project(infer_t_feats, target_head)
421	
422	    def alpha_qe(scores: np.ndarray, Q: np.ndarray, T: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
423	        """Expand each query with mean of its top-k matched targets, then re-rank."""
424	        new_scores = np.empty_like(scores)
425	        for i in range(len(Q)):
426	            top_k = np.argsort(-scores[i])[:AQE_K]
427	            expanded = AQE_ALPHA * Q[i] + (1 - AQE_ALPHA) * T[top_k].mean(axis=0)
428	            norm = np.linalg.norm(expanded)
429	            expanded = expanded / norm if norm > 0 else expanded
430	            new_scores[i] = expanded @ T.T
431	        return new_scores
432	
433	    def minmax_rows(m: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
434	        lo = m.min(axis=1, keepdims=True)
435	        hi = m.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)
436	        return (m - lo) / np.where(hi > lo, hi - lo, 1.0)
437	
438	    submission_rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
439	
440	    for pred_set in prediction_sets:
441	        ds = pred_set["ds"]
442	        query_ids = [qid for qid in sorted(pred_set["queries"]) if qid in proj_q]
443	        target_ids = [tid for tid in sorted(pred_set["targets"]) if tid in proj_t]
444	        if not query_ids or not target_ids:
445	            continue
446	
447	        Q = np.stack([proj_q[qid] for qid in query_ids])
448	        T = np.stack([proj_t[tid] for tid in target_ids])
449	
450	        dino_scores = Q @ T.T
451	        dino_scores = alpha_qe(dino_scores, Q, T)  # alpha-QE re-rank
452	
453	        if ds == "dataset1":
454	            q_ncc_idx = [ncc_q_idx[qid] for qid in query_ids]
455	            t_ncc_idx = [ncc_t_idx[tid] for tid in target_ids]
456	            ncc_sub = ncc_scores_d1[np.ix_(q_ncc_idx, t_ncc_idx)]
457	            scores = NCC_WEIGHT * minmax_rows(ncc_sub) + DINO_WEIGHT * minmax_rows(dino_scores)
458	        else:
459	            scores = dino_scores
460	
461	        for i, qid in enumerate(query_ids):
462	            ranked = [target_ids[j] for j in np.argsort(-scores[i])]
463	            submission_rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranked)})
464	
465	    buf = _io.StringIO()
466	    writer = _csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=["query_id", "target_id_ranking"])
467	    writer.writeheader()
468	    writer.writerows(submission_rows)
469	    print(f"\nGenerated {len(submission_rows)} submission rows.")
470	    return buf.getvalue()
471	
472	
473	def _find_data_root(mount: Path) -> Path:
474	    for p in sorted(mount.rglob("dataset1")):
475	        if p.is_dir():
476	            found = p.parent
477	            print(f"Found data root: {found}")
478	            return found
479	    raise RuntimeError(f"Could not find dataset1/ under {mount}. Top-level: {list(mount.iterdir())}")
480	
481	
482	@app.local_entrypoint()
483	def main(out: str = "rad_dino_submission.csv") -> None:
484	    print("Running RadDINO + alpha-QE baseline on Modal...")
485	    csv_content = run_rad_dino.remote()
486	    out_path = Path(out)
487	    out_path.write_text(csv_content, encoding="utf-8")
488	    print(f"Saved {len(csv_content.splitlines()) - 1} rows to {out_path}")
489
